title: dict: a shared dict iterator double-DECREFs di_dict in the exhaustion path -> negative refcount / use-after-free (dictiter_iternext_threadsafe fail:, Objects/dictobject.c:6159)
description: The free-threaded dict iterators (dict_keyiterator / dict_valueiterator / dict_itemiterator) funnel next() through dictiter_iternext_threadsafe. Its exhaustion path is `fail: di->di_dict = NULL; Py_DECREF(d);` -- a non-atomic clear + DECREF of the iterator's single owning reference to the dict -- and the caller dictiter_iternextkey reads `d = di->di_dict` with no lock. When one dict iterator is advanced by next() from multiple threads and they reach exhaustion together, two threads both read the same non-NULL d and both run the fail: Py_DECREF(d) -> the dict's refcount underflows -> double-free / use-after-free. This is the memory-unsafe crash face of the same di_dict race reported (and closed unfixed) in cpython#148873, distinct from the value-benign ma_values data race in the same function (TSAN-0026).
signature_note: No race signature emitted on purpose: TSAN-0053 is a CRASH (tsanNOPARSE -- an abort/segfault, not a TSan data-race report). Under TSan the same shared-iterator next() produces the `dictiter_iternext_threadsafe | dictiter_iternextkey` data race, which is ALREADY carried by TSAN-0026 (and suppressed as the race:dictiter_iternext gateway). Adding that signature here would collide with TSAN-0026 and mislabel the value-benign race. This entry is cataloged by report + crash_backtrace only.
sites: Objects/dictobject.c dictiter_iternext_threadsafe fail: (:6157-6159 in the fleet build) -- `di->di_dict = NULL; Py_DECREF(d);` non-atomically drops the iterator's one owning ref to the dict; two concurrent next() callers both do it
Objects/dictobject.c dictiter_iternextkey:5791 -- `PyDictObject *d = di->di_dict;` plain read of the reference the fail: path clears+decrefs
race_kind: shared iterator exhaustion: non-atomic clear+DECREF of di->di_dict (the iterator's single owning reference to the dict) across threads -> data race on di_dict AND a double-DECREF / use-after-free (refcount underflow). Memory-unsafe (crosses the gh-124397 'must not crash' bar), unlike the value-benign cursor races and unlike the ma_values data race (TSAN-0026) in the same function.
shared_state: dictiterobject.di_dict (the owning reference to the source dict) on a single shared dict iterator; the referenced dict's refcount
severity: HIGH (CRASHES): the double-DECREF drives the dict's refcount negative -> not just a TSan warning. repro.py (8 threads sharing one dict iterator, refilled on exhaustion) aborts ~8/8 runs on debug-ft-nojit with `_Py_NegativeRefcount` on the dict (Objects/dictobject.c:6159) or a corrupted dk_refcnt (`dictkeys_incref` immortal-refcount assert), near-instantly; on release-ft-nojit-o0 it SIGSEGVs (use-after-free) or occasionally deadlocks on the corrupted dict mutex (`PyMutex_Unlock: unlocking mutex that is not locked`). Reachable from pure Python. Real-world likelihood is low (sharing ONE dict iterator across threads is unusual), but the failure is a hard crash, not a benign wrong value. Free-threaded build only.
repro_reliability: On PLAIN free-threaded builds (no sanitizer): debug-ft-nojit aborts ~8/8 runs within seconds (`_Py_NegativeRefcount` on the dict at dictobject.c:6159, and the downstream `dictkeys_incref`/`new_dict`/`clear_freelist`/`validate_refcounts` corruption faces); release-ft-nojit-o0 SIGSEGVs (exit 139) or deadlocks. Under TSan the same shared-iterator next() reproduces the di_dict data race (cpython#148873's TSan report). Surfaced by fusil --tsan un-masking fleet-15 (inst-02, _colorize target -- the module is incidental; the shared iterator is a plain iter({...})).
suggested_fix: Consume the reference atomically so exactly one thread performs the DECREF, e.g. in the fail: path `PyDictObject *old = _Py_atomic_exchange_ptr(&di->di_dict, NULL); if (old != NULL) Py_DECREF(old);` -- and have the caller keep the dict alive for the duration of the lock-free walk (take a strong ref / the dict's critical section) so a sibling thread that wins the exchange cannot free the dict out from under an in-flight iteration. One fix covers keys/values/items since all three go through dictiter_iternext_threadsafe.
prior_art: PROVENANCE: the lock-free dict iterator dictiter_iternext_threadsafe was added by cpython#112075 / PR #115108 ('Iterating a dict shouldn't require locks', Dino Viehland, 2024-02) under colesbury's umbrella cpython#112075 ('Make dict objects thread-safe in --disable-gil builds'). PR #115108 explicitly 'allow[s] the iterator to be used from multiple threads simultaneously' and made the VALUE read safe (_Py_TryIncrefCompare/acquire_key_value) but COPIED the pre-existing `fail: di->di_dict = NULL; Py_DECREF(d)` exhaustion path in unchanged -> the double-free. CONTRACT: cpython#120496 ('Sequence iterator thread-safety', tested range/list/dict) DECIDED NOT to fix the value problem and only documented it (Doc/glossary.rst, PR #120685/#120706 merged; the 3 code-fix PRs #120534/#120540/#120591 closed unmerged) -- but colesbury and eendebakpt explicitly agreed on-issue that the contract is 'iterating using multiple threads will NOT crash the interpreter' (colesbury: 'Yes'), and eendebakpt warned of 'overflows inside the C code'. cpython#148873 reported THIS iterator's data-race face and was CLOSED AS A DUPLICATE OF cpython#120496 -- i.e. folded into the value-benign class, which is the gap: a double-free is a CRASH, exactly what #120496 puts on the not-acceptable side. Current main still double-frees. This report supplies the crash proof #148873 lacked (negative refcount / UAF / SIGSEGV), same escalation as TSAN-0045 (cpython#154043) and struct iter_unpack (cpython#154013). Distinct from TSAN-0026 (the ma_values plain-vs-atomic data race in the same function). gh api search found no OPEN issue for the dict-iterator double-free crash.
vehicle_examples: inst-02/python/_colorize-critical-tsanNOPARSE (tsanNOPARSE: it is an abort/assert, not a TSan data-race report)
confirmed_build: debug-ft-nojit (CPython 3.16.0a0, --disable-gil, main@a1d580430c8); also SIGSEGVs on release-ft-nojit-o0 -> NOT a debug-only artifact
confirmed_unfixed_on: 3.16.0a0 (fleet build main@a1d580430c8, current main; the fail: di_dict=NULL; Py_DECREF(d) lines date to 2004/2010/2016, pre-free-threading)
related: Memory-unsafe shared-iterator exhaustion (double-DECREF of the iterator's owning ref), same shape as TSAN-0045 (GenericAlias one-shot ga_iternext) and the bytes/str/struct iterator crashes (TSAN-0037/0038, cpython#154013). Distinct from the value-benign cursor iterators (TSAN-0044/0040) and from TSAN-0026 (ma_values data race, same function). Surfaced only via the --tsan un-masking profile because the plain dict-iterator DATA race is a suppressed gateway (race:dictiter_iternext), letting the rarer crash/abort face through.
notes: WHY THE UN-MASKING FLEET FOUND IT: the value-benign dict-iterator data race is a gateway (gateway_suppressions.txt: race:dictiter_iternext) -- normally it dominates and gets suppressed. With that suppressed, the rarer double-DECREF crash (an abort/segfault, NOT a TSan data-race report -> tsanNOPARSE) surfaced instead. FILEABLE: a crashing free-threading bug on current main; the prior data-race issue (cpython#148873) was closed without a fix, so a fresh issue framed as a memory-safety CRASH (with the pure-Python reproducer + the _Py_NegativeRefcount/UAF backtrace) is the right move, cross-referencing #148873. Per gh-124397 the iterator FT bar is 'must not crash' -- this crashes -> in scope. FILED as python/cpython#154130 (user posted, slightly edited the draft; cross-refs #148873 / gh-120496 / gh-112075). _colorize is incidental (the shared object was a plain iter({...}) in the fuzzer's op-mix).
